questions.question: What is the first step to add an icon to a Notion page?

options.text: Click the default image next to the page title
options.explanation: The guide says you click the default image next to the title to open the icon menu.

options.text: Open the Settings panel
options.explanation: Settings is not used for adding page icons.

options.text: Use a keyboard shortcut
options.explanation: There is no shortcut mentioned for adding an icon.

options.text: Edit the page properties
options.explanation: Page properties don’t include the icon selection.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a method for adding a cover photo to a Notion page?

options.text: Upload your own file
options.explanation: Uploading a file is a supported way to add a cover.

options.text: Choose an image from the Unsplash repository
options.explanation: Unsplash is one of the options listed for covers.

options.text: Ask Notion AI to create a cover photo based on a prompt
options.explanation: Notion AI can generate a cover from a prompt.

options.text: Use the Random button
options.explanation: The Random button applies to icons, not to cover photos.

questions.question: How can you obtain a cover photo using Notion AI?

options.text: Provide a descriptive prompt for the AI
options.explanation: The guide states you can ask Notion AI to create a cover based on a prompt.

options.text: Select a random image from the gallery
options.explanation: Random selection is for icons, not AI‑generated covers.

options.text: Upload a photo you previously saved
options.explanation: Uploading is a manual method, not AI‑generated.

options.text: Paste a direct image link from the web
options.explanation: Pasting a link adds an existing image, not an AI‑generated one.

questions.question: If you want an icon that matches your brand image, what should you do?

options.text: Upload your own icon file
options.explanation: The guide advises uploading your own icon (e.g., a company logo) to match branding.

options.text: Click the Random button until you find a match
options.explanation: Random selects emojis/icons at random and isn’t reliable for branding.

options.text: Choose an emoji from the menu
options.explanation: Emojis are generic and may not reflect a brand logo.

options.text: Select an image from Unsplash
options.explanation: Unsplash provides photos, not icons, and doesn’t guarantee brand consistency.

questions.question: Why are icons and cover photos useful for Notion pages?

options.text: They make each page unique and more digestible
options.explanation: The article states that icons and covers help turn text‑heavy documents into more digestible, unique pages.

options.text: They increase the page’s loading speed
options.explanation: Adding media generally adds load time, not speed.

options.text: They encrypt the page content for security
options.explanation: Icons and covers have no effect on encryption.

options.text: They reduce the amount of storage used by the page
options.explanation: Media actually consumes storage rather than reducing it.

questions.question: Where can you find the collection of over 10,000 templates referenced in the guide?

options.text: In the Template gallery
options.explanation: The guide points readers to browse the Template gallery for the 10,000+ templates.

options.text: In the Notion AI library
options.explanation: The AI library is not mentioned as a source for templates.

options.text: Under Settings → Templates
options.explanation: Templates are accessed via the gallery, not through Settings.

options.text: On the Help Center page
options.explanation: The Help Center provides guidance, not the template collection itself.

questions.question: Which sequence correctly describes how to replace an existing cover photo with a new one from Unsplash?

options.text: Inside the page, click Add cover → Change cover → select Unsplash → pick an image
options.explanation: The guide’s workflow: open the cover menu (Add cover or Change cover), choose Unsplash, then select an image.

options.text: Open Settings, go to Cover options, choose Unsplash, upload a new image
options.explanation: Cover changes are made from within the page, not the Settings panel.

options.text: Click the default icon next to the title, select Unsplash, then confirm
options.explanation: Unsplash is for covers, not for the page icon.

options.text: Paste an Unsplash URL into the page body, then click Convert to cover
options.explanation: The guide does not mention a ‘Convert to cover’ feature; you must use the cover menu.

questions.question: If an ad blocker stops the tutorial video from playing, what does the guide suggest you do?

options.text: Watch the video on YouTube using the provided link
options.explanation: The guide explicitly offers a YouTube link as an alternative when the ad blocker blocks the video.

options.text: Disable the ad blocker and reload the page
options.explanation: While disabling could work, the guide does not mention this as the recommended action.

options.text: Contact Notion support for a video file
options.explanation: The guide does not direct users to contact support for the video.

options.text: Refresh the page and try again
options.explanation: Refreshing isn’t suggested; the guide points to the YouTube version instead.
